extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a mid-level (≈0.45–0.55) with small noise.  
R2: There is one long plateau at a high level (≈0.8–0.9) occupying the middle of the series.  
R3: On one side of this long plateau (either left or right) there is an additional short, isolated spike to the same high level.  
R4: Between plateaus/spikes, the signal returns close to the mid-level baseline (not to very low values).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts and ends around a mid-level baseline (≈0.45–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: There is exactly one single long high plateau (≈0.8–0.9) in the middle of the series.  
R3: No additional high spikes exist outside this central plateau.  
R4: Outside the plateau the signal never drops to very low values (e.g., stays >≈0.4).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal starts and ends at a mid-level (≈0.45–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: There is one long low plateau (≈0.1–0.2) in the middle of the series.  
R3: On one or both sides of this long low plateau there are short, narrow dips that go even lower than the plateau.  
R4: Between dips and plateau, the signal quickly returns near the mid-level baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long middle segment where the values stay clearly higher than both the early and late segments (high plateau in the center).
R2: At least one of the edges (start or end) contains a short opposite bump: a brief high peak on an otherwise low side, or a brief low dip on an otherwise high side.
R3: The series begins and ends near the same intermediate baseline level (around the mid-range of all values).
R4: Away from the plateau and the short bump, the signal is relatively flat with only small noise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is a single long high plateau covering most of the central part of the series.
R2: Both the beginning and the end stay close to the same lower baseline level, clearly below the plateau.
R3: No strong local peaks or dips occur outside the main plateau (no pronounced extra bumps at the edges).
R4: Transitions between baseline and plateau are steep but occur only once up and once down.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is a long low plateau in the middle where values stay clearly lower than the early and late segments (deep central valley).
R2: Both the beginning and the end stay close to the same higher baseline level, above the central valley.
R3: In addition to the main valley, there is at least one extra sharp, narrow dip that is much lower than its immediate neighbors.
R4: Transitions from high baseline to the central low plateau and back are steep, forming a well-defined valley shape.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long central plateau whose values are clearly higher than the baseline (roughly 0.8 vs. 0.5), covering the middle of the series.  
R2: On at least one side (start or end) of the series there is an additional, much shorter peak above the baseline (a “small bump” before or after the long plateau).  
R3: Outside the peaks/plateau, the signal returns to a nearly constant baseline level around 0.45–0.5.  
R4: The series thus has at least two separated high segments: one long central high block and one short high block.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is a single long central plateau clearly above the baseline (around 0.8–0.9).  
R2: At the beginning and at the end of the series, the signal stays close to a constant baseline around 0.45–0.5.  
R3: Apart from the transitions into and out of the plateau, there are no additional pronounced peaks or dips.  
R4: The whole pattern is “low–high–low” with exactly one broad high segment.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is a long central valley clearly below the baseline (around 0.1–0.2 vs. baseline ~0.45–0.5).  
R2: The signal at the very beginning and near the end of the series is close to a constant higher baseline (around 0.45–0.5).  
R3: In addition to the long central valley, there is at least one extra narrow, deep dip from the baseline (a “small notch”) either before or after the central valley.  
R4: The series thus has at least two separated low segments: one long central low block and one short deeper dip.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long central plateau whose value is clearly different from the baseline (either clearly higher or clearly lower than 0.5) and lasts roughly from indices 40–110.  
R2: Outside this central plateau (at the beginning and end), the signal hovers near a common baseline around 0.45–0.55.  
R3: There is one additional, much shorter excursion (spike or dip) on one side (early or late) that is in the opposite direction of the long plateau (i.e., if the central part is high, the short one is low, and vice versa).  
R4: Apart from these structured excursions, the signal remains approximately flat with only small noise.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long high plateau, roughly between indices 45–105, with values around 0.8–0.9.  
R2: Before and after this plateau, the signal stays near a lower baseline around 0.47–0.50 without any additional pronounced spikes or dips.  
R3: The transitions from baseline to plateau and from plateau back to baseline are steep (almost vertical rises and drops).  
R4: No part of the series goes significantly below the baseline (e.g., below about 0.45).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long low plateau, roughly between indices 35–95, with values around 0.10–0.20.  
R2: Before and after this low plateau, the signal stays near an upper baseline around 0.45–0.50.  
R3: At least one short, sharp dip occurs outside the long low plateau, going clearly below the baseline and then quickly returning to it.  
R4: The central low plateau is flat compared to the surrounding baseline (only small noise), and its duration is much longer than any of the short dips.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts near a medium level (~0.5) for a short time.
R2: There is one long plateau segment whose level is clearly higher than the baseline (around 0.8) or clearly lower (around 0.1–0.2).
R3: The plateau occupies roughly the central part of the series (about indices 40–110).
R4: Outside this plateau, the series returns to and stays near the medium baseline (~0.5).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends near a medium level (~0.48–0.5).
R2: There is a single long plateau in the middle of the series (roughly indices 50–100).
R3: The plateau level is clearly higher than baseline (around 0.8–0.9).
R4: There are no additional large peaks or dips outside this central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has a long central plateau at a distinctly low level (around 0.1–0.2) between two higher baseline segments (~0.45–0.5).
R2: The low plateau occupies the middle of the series (roughly indices 40–100).
R3: There is at least one sharp, narrow dip (spike downward) below the baseline either before or after the low plateau.
R4: Apart from the central low plateau and these narrow dips, the rest of the series remains near the higher baseline (~0.45–0.5).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: There is a long central plateau where the signal stays clearly above 0.7 for a substantial portion of the middle of the series.  
R2: Outside this central high plateau, the signal stays near a medium baseline around 0.45–0.55.  
R3: Either just before the central plateau, or just after it (or both), there is a short, sharp excursion in the *opposite* direction: a brief high spike above 0.75 when the middle is low, or a brief deep dip below 0.25 when the middle is high.  
R4: The baseline level at the very start and at the very end of the series are similar (no lasting trend up or down across the whole window).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: There is a single dominant plateau in the middle of the series where the signal is high (about 0.8–0.9) and roughly flat.  
R2: Before and after this plateau, the signal is near a medium baseline (around 0.45–0.5) without any large spikes or dips.  
R3: The transitions into and out of the high plateau are steep but occur only once on each side (one main rise and one main fall).  
R4: The average level over the middle third of the series is significantly higher than over either the first or last third.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: There is a long central valley where the signal stays clearly below about 0.25 for a substantial portion of the middle of the series.  
R2: Before and after this valley, the signal returns to a higher baseline around 0.45–0.5.  
R3: Near the edges of the series (close to the beginning and/or close to the end), there are short, sharp extra dips that go well below the valley’s baseline (brief spikes downward).  
R4: The average level over the middle third of the series is significantly lower than over either the first or last third.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series has a long middle segment (roughly indices 40–110) whose values are clearly different from the rest of the series.  
R2: Outside that middle segment, the baseline level is around 0.45–0.5.  
R3: The middle segment level is far from the baseline (either clearly higher, ≈0.8, or clearly lower, ≈0.1–0.2).  
R4: At least one additional short peak or dip occurs near one end (beginning or end) that goes in the opposite direction of the middle segment (e.g., a short high bump near the start when the middle is low, or vice versa).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series has a single long plateau in the middle (roughly indices 50–100).  
R2: Before and after this plateau, the baseline is near 0.48.  
R3: The plateau is clearly higher than the baseline (around 0.8–0.9) and stays approximately flat.  
R4: There are no pronounced additional peaks or dips apart from the single rise to and fall from this plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series has one long low “valley” segment in the middle (roughly indices 40–100).  
R2: Before and after the valley, the signal is at a higher, flat baseline near 0.45–0.5.  
R3: The valley level is much lower than the baseline (around 0.1–0.2) and approximately flat.  
R4: There is at least one additional narrow, deep dip near the start or end that goes even lower than the valley.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The signal stays around a medium baseline (~0.45–0.5) at the very beginning and very end of the series.  
R2: There is one long plateau whose level is clearly different from the baseline (either noticeably higher or lower) lasting roughly from the first quarter to the last quarter of the series.  
R3: There is an additional short pulse (high or low) near one edge of the long plateau (close to either its start or its end), producing a “double-step” shape.  
R4: Outside the plateau and pulse, the signal has only small fluctuations around the baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The signal starts and ends at a medium baseline (~0.45–0.5) with small noise.  
R2: There is a single long high plateau (around 0.8–0.9) beginning near the middle of the first half and ending near the middle of the second half of the series.  
R3: The transitions from baseline to high plateau and from high plateau back to baseline are steep and monotonic (single sharp rise, single sharp fall).  
R4: No additional distinct peaks or dips appear outside this central high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The signal starts and ends at a higher baseline (~0.45–0.5) compared with its central minimum region.  
R2: There is one long low plateau (around 0.1–0.2) occupying the middle of the series, clearly lower than the start/end baseline.  
R3: One or two narrow, sharp dips occur near either the beginning or the end of the series (or both), dropping briefly below the local baseline before returning.  
R4: Aside from the central low plateau and these narrow dips, the signal segments are relatively flat with small noise.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.50 for most of the series.  
R2: There is one long plateau where the value is clearly above baseline (around 0.80) lasting from roughly time 30 to 110.  
R3: In addition to the long plateau, there is exactly one short excursion (spike or dip) on one side of the long plateau (either before or after it).  
R4: Outside these excursion segments, the signal quickly returns to the baseline level near 0.45–0.50.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.50 for most of the series.  
R2: There is one long plateau where the value is clearly above baseline (around 0.80–0.90) lasting from roughly time 50 to 100.  
R3: Apart from this single long high plateau, there are no other large spikes or dips; the rest of the signal stays near baseline.  
R4: The transitions from baseline to the long plateau and back are steep (sharp rise and sharp fall).
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The baseline level is around 0.45–0.50 for most of the series.  
R2: There is one long segment where the value is clearly below baseline (around 0.10–0.20) roughly between times 30 and 100.  
R3: On at least one side of this long low segment, there is a narrow deep dip that goes even lower than the long low segment.  
R4: Outside the long low segment and the narrow dips, the signal quickly returns to the baseline level near 0.45–0.50.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a mid-level (~0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is a long, wide plateau at a higher level (~0.8–0.85) in the central segment of the series.  
R3: Outside that high plateau (before and/or after it), the signal returns to the mid-level (~0.45–0.5).  
R4: Optionally, there may be one additional short spike or dip at the beginning or the end, but the dominant central feature is the high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts and ends at a mid-level (~0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is a single, long high plateau (~0.8–0.9) in the middle of the series.  
R3: The high plateau begins after about one third of the time axis and ends before about two thirds (i.e., roughly centered).  
R4: There are no extra pronounced spikes or dips apart from the single high plateau.
extracted_rules.class_2:
R1: The series starts and ends at a mid-level (~0.45–0.5).  
R2: There is one long, wide low plateau in the middle (~0.1–0.2), clearly lower than start/end.  
R3: There may be one or two additional narrow deep dips (values briefly much lower than the surrounding mid-level) either before or after the main low plateau.  
R4: Outside the low plateau and any narrow dips, the signal stays near the mid-level (~0.45–0.5).
